docs.title: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog
docs.text:
LinkML schema for the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog. The KEV catalog is the authoritative source of vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild, maintained by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

URI: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

Name: kev-catalog

docs.title: Class: KevCatalog
docs.text:
The CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog — the authoritative source of vulnerabilities confirmed exploited in the wild, maintained by CISA per Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01.

URI: kev_catalog:KevCatalog

 classDiagram
    class KevCatalog
    click KevCatalog href "../KevCatalog/"
      KevCatalog : catalog_version

      KevCatalog : count

      KevCatalog : date_released

      KevCatalog : title

      KevCatalog : vulnerabilities





        KevCatalog --> "*" KevEntry : vulnerabilities
        click KevEntry href "../KevEntry/"

docs.title: Class Properties
docs.text: Property Value Tree Root Yes

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Cardinality and Range Description Inheritance title 0..1  String The title of the KEV catalog direct catalog_version 0..1  String The version string of the KEV catalog release direct date_released 0..1  String The ISO 8601 timestamp at which this catalog version was released direct count 0..1  Integer The total number of vulnerabilities in this catalog version direct vulnerabilities *  KevEntry The list of known exploited vulnerabilities direct

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• BOD 22-01, "Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities", was issued under Section 3553(b)(2) of title 44, U.S. Code by the Secretary of Homeland Security. It is a compulsory direction to all FCEB departments and agencies.
  • CISA strongly recommends all organizations — including state, local, tribal and territorial (SLTT) governments and private sector — prioritize remediation of KEV catalog entries as part of their vulnerability management plan, even though BOD 22-01 itself only binds FCEB agencies.
  • BOD 22-01 does not apply to: national security systems (as defined by statute), systems operated by the Department of Defense, or systems operated by the Intelligence Community.

docs.text:
• https://www.cisa.gov/directives
  • https://www.cisa.gov/binding-operational-directive-22-01

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• Three criteria must all be met for inclusion: (1) assigned CVE ID, (2) reliable evidence of active exploitation in the wild, and (3) clear remediation guidance (vendor update or EOL removal).
  • BOD 22-01 focuses on a small, high-signal subset of all vulnerabilities: as of 2022-07-15 the catalog contained 787 KEVs from a universe of ~179,383 CVEs, and approximately 20 of those KEVs were the most prevalent across the entire federal enterprise.

    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

  date_added:

    required: true
  short_description:
    name: short_description
    description: A brief description of the vulnerability.
    comments:
    - 'KEV inclusion criterion #2 requires reliable evidence that the vulnerability
      has been actively exploited in the wild. Active exploitation includes attempted
      and successful exploitation; scanning, security research, and public PoC alone
      do not qualify.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - shortDescription
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: dcterms:description
    alias: short_description
    owner: KevEntry
    domain_of:
    - KevEntry
    range: string
    required: true
  required_action:
    name: required_action
    description: The remediation action required, e.g. "Apply updates per vendor instructions."
    comments:
    - 'BOD 22-01 specifies two acceptable actions: (1) apply updates per vendor instructions,
      or (2) remove the product from agency networks if it is end-of-life or cannot
      otherwise be updated. Temporary mitigations and workarounds may be applied while
      awaiting a vendor patch.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - requiredAction
    rank: 1000
    alias: required_action
    owner: KevEntry
    domain_of:
    - KevEntry
    range: string
    required: true
  due_date:
    name: due_date
    description: The date (YYYY-MM-DD) by which federal civilian executive branch
      (FCEB) agencies must remediate the vulnerability per BOD 22-01.
    comments:
    - Deadline set by CISA. Under BOD 19-02, internet-accessible critical vulnerabilities
      (CVSS >9.0) must be remediated within 15 calendar days and high vulnerabilities
      (7.0-9.0) within 30 calendar days of initial detection; BOD 22-01 due dates
      may be tighter and apply to all agency systems, not only internet-accessible
      ones.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - dueDate
    rank: 1000
    alias: due_date
    owner: KevEntry
    domain_of:
    - KevEntry
    range: IsoDate
    required: true
  known_ransomware_campaign_use:

    description: Whether the vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part
      of a ransomware campaign.
    notes:
    - CISA encourages organizations with evidence of exploited vulnerabilities not
      listed in the KEV to submit them to vulnerability@mail.cisa.dhs.gov.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

docs.text: Value Meaning Description ACTIVE None Vulnerability is actively maintained and published REJECTED None CVE ID was rejected and should not be used DISPUTED None The vulnerability details are disputed by a party RESERVED None CVE ID is reserved but details are not yet published DEPRECATED None Entry has been superseded or withdrawn
